evidence.scientific_clarity: Both ROC and PRC panels are present with correct axes, diagonal/prevalence reference lines, and per-method AUROC/AP annotations in legends as required by brief.
evidence.typography: Script sets no pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (non-editable bitmap) fonts; DejaVu Sans default used throughout instead of a publication serif/sans.
evidence.palette: Four colors used (#888888, #2196F3, #FF9800, #E53935); gray+blue+orange+red is partially CB-safe but red/orange combination is problematic for deuteranopia; no directional green/red discipline applied to performance curves.
evidence.layout_density: Square aspect ratio with equal axes set to -0.02..1.02 uses space well; however two equal-sized square panels in a 9x4.2 fig leave narrow margins and legend placement in lower-right/lower-left is sensible but the prevalence label inside PRC legend adds minor clutter.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and correctly identifies all elements; n_pos and n_neg stated numerically; 'OursModel achieves highest' claim is present; minor issue: legend includes prevalence baseline mixed with method entries in PRC panel.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF exists but pdffonts shows Type 3 (bitmap) fonts, not Type 42 TrueType; script omits 'matplotlib.rcParams[pdf.fonttype]=42'; text is not selectable/searchable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: All four required files exist and are non-trivial: figure.pdf (25 KB), figure.png (181 KB), caption.tex (607 B), gen_figure.py (2.6 KB); bonus figure.svg also present.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption provides sample sizes (n_pos=120, n_neg=180), explains both panels and both reference lines, identifies the best method by name with a directional claim; meets reviewer expectations for a methods figure.

overall_strengths: Complete two-panel ROC+PRC figure with correct reference lines, per-method AUROC/AP annotations, and well-structured caption matching the brief exactly.
All required output files are present and non-trivial; caption is quantitative and names the best-performing method explicitly.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 is not set, producing Type 3 bitmap fonts in the PDF — text is not editable or selectable, and the default DejaVu Sans font is not publication-quality.
Color palette uses red (#E53935) and orange (#FF9800) adjacently, which is problematic for red-green colorblind readers; no explicit colorblind-safe palette (e.g., Wong/Okabe-Ito) was applied.
